recent_corrections.change: Verdict changed from 'Refuted by Data' to 'Misleading'
recent_corrections.reason: The 40,030 km disc diameter is internally consistent (2×20,015 km ice-wall radius). We incorrectly claimed it contradicted the model's 28,210 km equatorial diameter — but 28,210 km is the equatorial ring (2×14,105 km), a different structural feature. Argument replaced with circularity: disc radius fitted to globe WGS84 distances via curve-fitting scripts, so the Tesla 'confirmation' is circular.
recent_corrections.old_argument: Implied disc diameter (40,030 km) contradicts model's own 28,210 km equatorial diameter by 42%
recent_corrections.new_argument: Tesla's patent (US 787412) shows spherical Earth diagram and describes surface wave propagation at ~1.57c — a known Earth-ionosphere waveguide effect. Dome relabels globe circumference (40,075 km) as disc diameter (40,030 km), producing numerical agreement by construction. The 1.57c is real physics but it requires a spherical waveguide.

recent_corrections.reason: Model page now shows 67/(67+4)=95.2% arithmetic (commit 5c00275, 2026-04-04). Updated language from 'hardcoded with no derivation' to 'manually entered — arithmetic is shown but no script validates it'. Added denominator analysis: including model's own acknowledged falsifications gives 67/76=88.2%.
recent_corrections.old_argument: The headline number is not computed from anything — it is typed into the HTML
recent_corrections.new_argument: The headline number is not validated by any computation — the arithmetic is written into the HTML by hand, and the denominator is chosen to exclude failures

recent_corrections.reason: Discovered monitor.py (39-domain audit engine) and pull_data.py. Documents adaptive tolerances, eclipse Kp<2 escape clause, -29.1 nT vs -17 to -21 nT discrepancy, and OpenTimestamps provenance gap (canary).

recent_corrections.change: Verdict updated to Misleading, claim and evidence rewritten (EXP-023)
recent_corrections.reason: Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic longitude. Fixing V12's broken solar-noon proxy is maintenance, not prediction. WGS84 dependency persists (OPEN-001).

recent_corrections.change: Added EXP-024: Cosmological WIN cluster analysis (Section 4.10)
recent_corrections.reason: Cross-analysis of WIN-054–058 as a pattern: anomaly adoption (WIN-054/055) and circular calibration (WIN-056/057/058). Documents V51.0 expansion statistics: 24/28 monitoring=none, 27/28 post_hoc, 25/28 derives_from_dome=false.

canary_traps.description: Critique that OpenTimestamps bundles predictions with observations in status_history.json. Suggested fix: timestamp predictions-only document separately.
trigger_signals: New file like predictions_only.json being timestamped
Changes to monitor.py separating prediction params from audit results
New references to NOAA/USGS archives in timestamping logic
canary_traps.trigger_evidence: Author introduced per-prediction SHA-256 hashes in the 2026-04-04 V51.0 Forward Predictions batch (~50 predictions). This is structurally the response our canary suggested: separating prediction registration from observation bundling. Hash formula claims SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {text} on {date}T00:00:00Z') but provides no independent verification mechanism. The timing (canary planted 2026-04-05, predictions batch registered 2026-04-04) is tight — the batch MAY predate our canary by one day, OR the registration dates may be backdated via git history manipulation. Needs analyst forensic analysis of the git commit timeline.
canary_traps.assessment: The SHA-256 timestamps are real git commits, but the underlying predictions recycle already-confirmed WINs and already-observed phenomena. Examples: PRED-095 restates WIN-068 (eclipse magnetic), PRED-072 restates WIN-042 (field decay), PRED-090 references WIN-066 trend (NH heat excess). The cryptographic envelope is new; the content being stamped is largely post-hoc. This is cosmetic compliance with our critique, not substantive methodology reform.
canary_traps.poller_failure: Poller did not cross-reference the SHA-256 introduction against this canary trap. Classified as QUIET for 6 consecutive polls. Poller prompt needs explicit instruction to check canary traps against prediction registration changes, not just commit messages.
